channel.title: NDTV News Search Records Found 1000
channel.description: NDTV.com provides the latest information from and in-depth coverage of India and the world. Find breaking news, India news, top stories, elections, politics, business, cricket, movies, lifestyle, health, live TV, videos, photos and more.

item.title: PM Modi To Participate In G7 Summit In France Today

media:content.media:description: PM Modi is visiting the French city at the invitation of President Macron

item.description: New Delhi, June 16 (IANS) Prime Minister Narendra Modi will arrive in Evian, France, on Tuesday on a two-day visit to participate in the G7 Summit, marking the third and final leg of his two-nation tour.
item.content:encoded: New Delhi, June 16 (IANS) Prime Minister Narendra Modi will arrive in Evian, France, on Tuesday on a two-day visit to participate in the G7 Summit, marking the third and final leg of his two-nation tour.

item.title: UK To Supply Nuclear Fuel To Ukraine, Imposes Fresh Sanctions On Russia

media:content.media:description: Starmer's office said the energy deal would "power Ukraine for the next two years".

item.description: The UK will supply enriched uranium to Ukraine for its nuclear power stations and impose new sanctions against Russia, Prime Minister Keir Starmer said ahead of a G7 summit session Tuesday.
item.content:encoded: The UK will supply enriched uranium to Ukraine for its nuclear power stations and impose new sanctions against Russia, Prime Minister Keir Starmer said ahead of a G7 summit session Tuesday.

item.title: 8 Killed After B-52 Bomber Crashes At US Base In California

media:content.media:description: There was no immediate information on whether anyone was hurt.

item.description: A B-52 bomber crashed shortly after takeoff Monday morning at a U.S. Air Force base in California's Mojave Desert, officials said.
item.content:encoded: <iframe title="Player" class="head-story__img img__full lazyload" height="300" scrolling=no frameborder=no src="about:blank" data-src="https://www.ndtv.com/video/embed-player/id/1113666/play-via-app-wap?pWidth=100&pHeight=100&embed_type=story&site=classic&autostart=1"> </iframe>A B-52 bomber crashed shortly after takeoff Monday morning at a U.S. Air Force base in California's Mojave Desert, officials said.

item.title: Amid G7, Firing On Indians In Iran, PM's Sidebar With Donald Trump In France

media:content.media:description: The White House had announced the meeting last week.

item.description: The G-7 meeting comes days after the death of three Indian mariners on a commercial ship targeted by the US navy in the Strait of Hormuz.
item.content:encoded: The G-7 meeting comes days after the death of three Indian mariners on a commercial ship targeted by the US navy in the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Iran Will Never Have Nuclear Weapons, Not Today And Not Tomorrow: Netanyahu

media:content.media:description: Netanyahu said that we saved the Israel from the threat of "total destruction".

item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Monday evening defended his actions, emphasising that "Iran will not have nuclear weapons, with or without an agreement".
item.content:encoded: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Monday evening defended his actions, emphasising that "Iran will not have nuclear weapons, with or without an agreement".

item.title: After Putin Rejected G7 Talks Offer, Zelensky Now Seeks Meeting In US

media:content.media:description: Putin has repeatedly rejected offers for a face-to-face meeting with Zelensky.

item.description: The Ukrainian leader said he made the proposal in a call with US President Donald Trump, and was waiting to see what came of it.
item.content:encoded: The Ukrainian leader said he made the proposal in a call with US President Donald Trump, and was waiting to see what came of it.

item.title: Israeli Forces Won't Leave Lebanon, Syria, Gaza: Netanyahu After US-Iran Deal

media:content.media:description: Netanyahu said that country's forces would remain in Lebanon, Gaza and Syria.

item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said on Monday that the country's forces would remain in Gaza, Lebanon and Syria "for as long as necessary," hours after a deal was announced between Iran and the US to end the Middle East war.
item.content:encoded: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said on Monday that the country's forces would remain in Gaza, Lebanon and Syria "for as long as necessary," hours after a deal was announced between Iran and the US to end the Middle East war.

item.title: Russian Drone Hits Zoo In Ukraine's Kharkiv, Kills Animals

media:content.media:description: There were no casualties among staff or visitors.

item.description: A Russian drone struck enclosures at the zoo in Ukraine's second-largest city of Kharkiv on Monday, killing rabbits and inflicting stress on other animals, prosecutors said.
item.content:encoded: A Russian drone struck enclosures at the zoo in Ukraine's second-largest city of Kharkiv on Monday, killing rabbits and inflicting stress on other animals, prosecutors said.

item.title: Netanyahu Says Will Run In Upcoming Israeli Elections
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Monday said he intended to run in elections scheduled for later this year, as he faced domestic criticism over his handling of the Middle East war and its aftermath.
item.content:encoded: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Monday said he intended to run in elections scheduled for later this year, as he faced domestic criticism over his handling of the Middle East war and its aftermath.

item.title: Over 1,000 Civilians Died In Sudan Drone Strikes In First 5 Months Of 2026

media:content.media:description: The war killed at least 59,000 people over the course of three years

item.description: UN High Commissioner for Human Rights Volker Turk said his office has documented a "sharp increase" in drone attacks as well as rape and sexual violence in the Sudan war, now in its fourth year.
item.content:encoded: UN High Commissioner for Human Rights Volker Turk said his office has documented a "sharp increase" in drone attacks as well as rape and sexual violence in the Sudan war, now in its fourth year.

item.title: Option Trading, Greenshoe: What's Next For SpaceX Stock After Hit IPO

media:content.media:description: SpaceX has not set a date for its next earnings report.

item.description: The IPO includes a so-called greenshoe option, a standard feature of most large US stock market listings that acts like a safety valve that keeps the stock price from going crazy one way or another in its first month.
item.content:encoded: The IPO includes a so-called greenshoe option, a standard feature of most large US stock market listings that acts like a safety valve that keeps the stock price from going crazy one way or another in its first month.

item.title: No Livestreaming, Curfews: How UK's Social Media Ban For Under-16s Will Work

media:content.media:description: The government will also be looking in more detail at overnight curfews.

item.description: The government said regulator Ofcom would conduct a rapid study to establish the best way to verify if someone is over 16, and would have a new enforcement strategy and funding.
item.content:encoded: The government said regulator Ofcom would conduct a rapid study to establish the best way to verify if someone is over 16, and would have a new enforcement strategy and funding.

item.title: Microsoft Sued By Shareholders Over Defrauding Them, Inflating Stock Price

media:content.media:description: It is common for shareholders to sue companies for alleged securities fraud

item.description: About $357 billion of market value was erased, and Microsoft's stock suffered its biggest one-day decline in nearly six years.
item.content:encoded: About $357 billion of market value was erased, and Microsoft's stock suffered its biggest one-day decline in nearly six years.

item.title: "Ships Starting To Move": Trump Claims Oil Tankers Crossing Strait Of Hormuz

media:content.media:description: The deal is expected to be signed on Friday in Switzerland.

item.description: Pakistan announced on Sunday that the United States and Iran had agreed to an "immediate and permanent termination" of military operations, but the text of their peace deal has yet to be released.
item.content:encoded: <iframe title="Player" class="head-story__img img__full lazyload" height="300" scrolling=no frameborder=no src="about:blank" data-src="https://www.ndtv.com/video/embed-player/id/1113659/play-via-app-wap?pWidth=100&pHeight=100&embed_type=story&site=classic&autostart=1"> </iframe>Pakistan announced on Sunday that the United States and Iran had agreed to an "immediate and permanent termination" of military operations, but the text of their peace deal has yet to be released.

item.title: Unpaid Debts, Undeclared Work? Sweden Can Now Legally Kick Out Immigrants

media:content.media:description: The law does not specify what types of behaviours are deemed unacceptable

item.description: The law has been criticised by the opposition and human rights advocacy groups as arbitrary because decisions would be taken on behaviour that has not been deemed criminal.
item.content:encoded: The law has been criticised by the opposition and human rights advocacy groups as arbitrary because decisions would be taken on behaviour that has not been deemed criminal.

item.title: US-Iran War LIVE Updates: JD Vance To Lead Delegation For Iran Peace Deal Signing In Switzerland
item.description: US-Iran War LIVE Updates: Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesperson Esmaeil Baqaei reportedly denied that a memorandum of understanding would be signed.
item.content:encoded: US-Iran War LIVE Updates: Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesperson Esmaeil Baqaei reportedly denied that a memorandum of understanding would be signed.

item.title: Donald Trump Says Strait Of Hormuz Will Be 'Completely Open' On Friday

media:content.media:description: Trump hailed the deal made with Iran.

item.description: Donald Trump Says Strait Of Hormuz Will Be 'Completely Open' On Friday
item.content:encoded: Donald Trump Says Strait Of Hormuz Will Be 'Completely Open' On Friday

item.title: White House Hopes US-Iran Deal Text To Be Released This Week: JD Vance

media:content.media:description: The deal marked the biggest breakthrough towards resolving the war.

item.description: Vance also said the United States expects the economically vital Strait of Hormuz would be open without tolls on a long-term basis.
item.content:encoded: Vance also said the United States expects the economically vital Strait of Hormuz would be open without tolls on a long-term basis.

item.title: He Tortured, Raped Bashar Al-Assad's Opponents For Years. Then Came Judgment Day

media:content.media:description: Rafik A was convicted of 19 counts of crimes against humanity against eight victims.

item.description: The court said victims were "handcuffed and blindfolded, beaten with various objects and kicked for prolonged periods, folded up inside a car tyre, hung upside down, or electrocuted, often being forced to be naked."
item.content:encoded: The court said victims were "handcuffed and blindfolded, beaten with various objects and kicked for prolonged periods, folded up inside a car tyre, hung upside down, or electrocuted, often being forced to be naked."

item.title: Trump Celebrates 80th Birthday As White House Turns Into Cage Fight Arena

media:content.media:description: A series of UFC combatants paid tribute to the president after winning

item.description: In unprecedented scenes in the revered seat of American democracy, Trump sat with thousands of cheering fans as Ultimate Fighting Championship (UFC) combatants beat each other to a pulp.
item.content:encoded: In unprecedented scenes in the revered seat of American democracy, Trump sat with thousands of cheering fans as Ultimate Fighting Championship (UFC) combatants beat each other to a pulp.
